text: Corticosteroid injection decreases Morton's neuroma pain more than control, with a weighted mean difference of -5.3 points on a 100-point VAS scale.
Manipulation and mobilisation techniques reduce Morton's neuroma pain compared to control, with a mean difference of -15.3 points on a 100-point VAS scale.
Corticosteroid injection is significantly more effective than footwear modification and padding alone for Morton's neuroma treatment success.
Extracorporeal shockwave therapy does not show a statistically significant pain reduction compared to control for Morton's neuroma.
Corticosteroid injections and manipulation/mobilisation are the non-surgical interventions with the strongest evidence for Morton's neuroma pain reduction.
No single non-surgical intervention has been established as a gold-standard treatment for Morton's neuroma based on high-quality evidence.
